step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("level of spinal cord termination in adults")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on reviewing the newly retrieved evidence about the typical level where the spinal cord ends in adults, the information remains consistent with prior summaries—most sources indicate that in adults, the spinal cord terminates near the lower border of the first lumbar vertebra (L1). Specific studies like [3], [10], and [14] confirm this fact empirically measured through anatomy descriptions and cross-sectional views. No conflicting data suggesting other locations were found among reviewed references.
Given this alignment of evidence supporting the existing view held confidently by many professionals, including myself, I reaffirm agreement with our previous stance.
\boxed{\text{B}}
